headline: Sabah-Sarawak Budget Unity Talks Collapse Over Priorities
context: Attempts to present unified Borneo budget proposal collapsed in February 2026 when GRS and GPS disagreed on priority allocations worth RM12 billion.

cards.big: GRS and GPS failed to agree on unified RM12 billion Borneo budget proposal
cards.sub: Sabah wanted infrastructure priority while Sarawak pushed revenue autonomy

cards.big: GPS demanded 40% allocation for energy sovereignty and digital economy
cards.sub: GRS countered that Sabah basic infrastructure gap required at least 55% share

cards.big: Sabah GDP per capita of RM24,800 is 30% below Sarawak RM35,200
cards.sub: Development gap between two Borneo states has widened since 2015

cards.big: Federal Treasury offered RM8 billion total well below the RM12 billion ask
cards.sub: Both states submitted separate proposals weakening their bargaining position

cards.big: Failed unity tests whether Borneo states can overcome internal divisions for shared goals
Sabah-Sarawak budget talks collapsed because unity requires shared priorities, not just shared grievances.
